section_1: Social Security retirement benefits are available to workers who have earned 40 work credits, which typically requires about 10 years of covered employment. Full retirement age is 67 for those born in 1960 or later; reduced early benefits are available from age 62.
section_2: Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) benefits require medical evidence of a qualifying impairment that is expected to last at least 12 months or result in death, and that prevents the applicant from engaging in substantial gainful activity.
section_3: Survivors benefits may be paid to the spouse and minor children of a deceased worker. A surviving spouse may qualify starting at age 60, or age 50 if disabled.
section_4: Supplemental Security Income (SSI) is a needs-based programme for individuals with limited income and resources, funded from general tax revenue rather than Social Security taxes.
section_5: Medicare initial enrolment runs from three months before to three months after the month a person turns 65. Part A is usually premium-free for those with sufficient work credits; Part B has a monthly premium that may vary with income.
